codeReview.summary: The close-accounting change correctly limits app-exit decisions to authoritative user-window labels and adds regression tests covering multi-user, last-user, and non-user close cases. I found no blocking or non-blocking implementation issues in scope for this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The window-lifecycle worker did not follow the skill's manual desktop-surface validation step for this backend-only close-accounting feature, but the deviation appears justified because the change is fully covered by Rust close-accounting tests and the mission already separated desktop-surface validation into dedicated validation features. The skill should explicitly allow automated-only completion for backend-only close-accounting work when no UI behavior changes are introduced.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoffs/2026-03-17T10-24-21-337Z__window-close-accounting__2c124e28-57c8-4895-8fb1-bc1fe73d5c1a.json skillFeedback.deviations[0]; .factory/skills/window-lifecycle-worker/SKILL.md step 8 requires manual Tauri validation for every feature.

summary: Reviewed feature window-close-accounting at commit 00c946883781fb1f23e03d6d66091bbf3362ef7f. The implementation and added Rust regressions satisfy the feature requirements, and no code issues were identified.
